job_125.name: p0-opt001-test
job_125._note: Initial OPT-001 binary used cached_tokens / prompt_cache_hit_tokens fields; DashScope returns prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens (nested). Bug fixed in v2.

job_126.name: p0-opt001-v2
job_126._note: Cancelled mid-tts after 740s. retranslate stage stuck on a single segment; sufficient data already collected. No cache hit because (a) translate uses 5 segments only and (b) kimi-k2.5 retranslate prompts vary per attempt.

job_128.name: p0-opt003-tools-on
job_128._note: DashScope kimi-k2.5 supports OpenAI-compatible function calling perfectly. 1 high-quality merge suggestion (segments 9160+9161, confidence 0.85). 0 fallbacks to legacy prompt path.

job_129.name: p0-opt002-judge-on
job_129._note: Segment 4 judge verdict 'retry' correctly identified the same segment that was stuck in retranslation loop in baseline (10+ retries). Judge agent agrees with the symptom (missing 'monitoring' translation) and would, in non-observe mode, trigger a targeted retranslation. Cache hit on judge calls (512/6023 = 8.5%) confirms OPT-001 infrastructure is end-to-end working.

OPT-001._caveat: 5-segment short video is a worst case for cache: translate fires only 5 calls and DashScope appears to need 5+ identical-prefix calls to engage cache (verified by direct curl). Long videos (79min/626 segments) and judge calls (which fire per-segment) both naturally accumulate cache hits over time. Recommend re-validating against the MIT 6.824 long-video benchmark.
followups: OPT-001-followup-1: reproduce cache hit ratio target on the 79min benchmark video
OPT-001-followup-2: capture streaming usage from doChatStream for retranslate_thinking calls (currently shows token=0 in metric)

OPT-002._human_verify: Segment 4 verdict 'retry' with issue 'missing monitoring reference' was independently confirmed by inspecting the source vs target text — this is a legitimate semantic loss the drift-only baseline did not catch.
OPT-002.ready_for_decision_path_integration: Defer to OPT-201 SegmentAgent refactor; observe-only mode keeps the main pipeline contract unchanged.

comparison_summary_vs_baseline._speedup_caveat: Speedup is primarily due to LUCK in retranslation convergence (segment 4 took ~10 retries in baseline, ~5 here). OPT-001/002/003 are NOT performance optimisations — they add observability + structured outputs + judge. Speedup will normalise to ~baseline on average over many runs.
new_signals: per-operation token attribution (translate / retranslate / review / summary / judge)
per-model cost-USD foundation (waiting for OPT-101 OTEL price-table)
strict_parse_failed counter (segment_review tool path = 0 over 2 jobs)
